title: Hedge monitor classifies tool-call-heavy responses as empty — tool_results not in classify context
body: The hedge monitor's classify context includes tool_calls (names of tools called) but not tool_results (what those tools produced). When the assistant's response is primarily tool work — edits, writes, commits, builds — the text portion is a brief summary. The classifier sees tool names and a terse text response, concludes 'the assistant provided no response', and flags incorrectly. Observed: 'The assistant provided no response at all' on a turn that edited files, ran builds, and committed. The work was in tool results the classifier never saw. Fix: add tool_results to the hedge monitor's classify.context array, or teach the classify prompt that tool_calls represent substantive work even when assistant_text is brief.
resolved_by: commit 2745cf9 — tool_results added to hedge classify context, classify template updated to treat tool results as evidence of substantive work
